4. filter api_key for `ip_address` equal to `85.49.56.78`: set `search` equal to `ip_address:85.49.56.78` and set `searchFields` equal to `ip_address:=`

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a batch operations.

**USAGE NOTES** :
When you want to use a API command inside a batch operation, every API command parameter of API command must be in the operation's body.
If the single API operation need one or more parameters in the URI (eg. the id), this parameter must be placed inside the single operation body using **snake_case** syntax when used inside a batch operation.


For example you can refer to the **Product.Store** command, as you can see in the URI the **storeId** parameter is needed:

    /stores/{storeId}/products

and the request body look like this:

    {
        "product_id": "your product id",
        "variant_id": "your variant id",
        "title": "T-shirt",
        "description": "T-shirt",
        "category": "T-shirt",
        "status": "enabled",
        "category_med": "man",
        "category_upper": "child",
        "product_url": "http://www.mystore.com/supercool-tshirt",
        "product_image_url": "http://www.mystore.com/images/supercool-tshirt.jpg",
        "brand": "SuperCool brand",
        "price": 56.76,
        "code": "shd8s76d-23d3",
        "attributes": [
            {
            "id": 2,
            "value": "red"
            }
        ]
    }


To use the same API command inside a batch operation, we need to move the **storeId** parameter inside the body as a new parameter:

    {
        "store_id": "YOUR STORE ID HERE",
        "product_id": "your product id",
        "variant_id": "your variant id",
        "title": "T-shirt",
        "description": "T-shirt",
        "category": "T-shirt",
        "status": "enabled",
        "category_med": "man",
        "category_upper": "child",
        "product_url": "http://www.mystore.com/supercool-tshirt",
        "product_image_url": "http://www.mystore.com/images/supercool-tshirt.jpg",
        "brand": "SuperCool brand",
        "price": 56.76,
        "code": "shd8s76d-23d3",
        "attributes": [
            {
            "id": 2,
            "value": "red"
            }
        ]
    }

This logic is valid for every API command available inside a batch operation.

Commands available :

for **CustomField** :

    CustomField.Store

for **Stores / Product** :

    Stores.Product.Store
    Stores.Product.Update
    Stores.Product.Delete

for **Stores / Cart** :

    Stores.Cart.Store
    Stores.Cart.Update
    Stores.Cart.Delete

get.description: Use this endpoint to generate an email sent report of campaign. This feature is available only on email campaign.
Use this endpoint to download a email sent report of campaign. This feature is available only on email campaign.

Use this endpoint to get paginated campaigns.

You can filter for a specific campaign by `name`, `status`, `email.id`, `sms.id` and `tags.id` properties.
You can retrieve message data information passing `include` property with `email`, or `sms` value.

For `status` property you should pass one of these values `Draft`, `Ready`, `Sending`, `Paused`, `Pending Approval`, `Sent` or `Failed`.

Examples:

1. filter campaigns for `name` like to `MyCampaign`: set `search` equal to `name:MyCampaign`,
3. filter campaigns for `status` equal to `Ready`: set `search` equal to `status:Ready`,
4. filter campaigns for `status` equal to `Sending` or `Ready`: set `search` equal to `status:Sending,Ready` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in`
5. filter campaigns for `status` equal to `Sent` or `Draft` and `name` equal to `MyCampaign` field: set `search` equal to `status:Sent,Draft;name:MyCampaign` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in;name:like` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
6. filter campaigns for `email.id` equal to `124`: set `search` equal to `email.id:124` and set `searchFields` equal to `email.id:=`,
7. filter campaigns for `email.id` equal to `124` or equal to `421`: set `search` equal to `email.id:124,421` and set `searchFields` equal to `email.id:in`,
8. filter campaigns for `sms.id` equal to `124`: set `search` equal to `sms.id:124` and set `searchFields` equal to `sms.id:=`,
9. filter campaigns for `sms.id` equal to `124` or equal to `421`: set `search` equal to `sms.id:124,421` and set `searchFields` equal to `sms.id:in`,
10. filter campaigns with no sms or email defined: set `search` equal to `sms.id:0;email.id:0` and set `searchFields` equal to `sms.id:=;email.id:=` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
9. filter campaigns for `split_test.id` equal to `124` or equal to `421`: set `search` equal to `split_test.id:124,421` and set `searchFields` equal to `split_test.id:in`,
11. filter campaigns for `tags.id` equal to `124` or equal to `421`: set `search` equal to `tags.id:124,421` and set `searchFields` equal to `tags.id:in`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Return email, sms, label_tags or split_test data

post.summary: Create a new cart on store
post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new cart on the given store. If passed products not in the catalog, will not be added on cart. **R.O.I. MEASUREMENT** You can measure the orders generated from a campaign using our R.O.I. tracking javascript. This script must be included in your website public frontend and it will get three custom parameters set from the console link traking engine. Those parameters will be stored in a cookie on the visitor browser mapping the source campaign or automation message from were he is coming. This cookie will last a for X days (default 3 days) and will store those information. If the visitor return on the website and make an order you can read this cookie and send back those information through the **source** and **source_id** parameters of the Carts commands. This will make us able to measure track the the orders source if the user is coming in from a link in a campaign with R.O.I. Traking enabled. **LINK SCRIPT** Use script tag to add our script on your website. If you have a dedicated console, substitute [API_URL] with the url of your api. For example *https://api.4dem.it/c4rt/js/ect.min.js* [API_URL]/c4rt/js/ect.min.js **COOKIE NAME** ectDem **COOKIE PARAMETERS** { "id": 23, "mode": "campaign" }

get.description:
Get cart

Return Cart information

put.description: Use this endpoint to update a cart on the given store. **R.O.I. MEASUREMENT** You can measure the orders generated from a campaign using our R.O.I. tracking javascript. This script must be included in your website public frontend and it will get three custom parameters set from the console link traking engine. Those parameters will be stored in a cookie on the visitor browser mapping the source campaign or automation message from were he is coming. This cookie will last a for X days (default 3 days) and will store those information. If the visitor return on the website and make an order you can read this cookie and send back those information through the **source** and **source_id** parameters of the Carts commands. this will make us able to measure track the the orders source if the user is coming in from a link in a campaign with R.O.I. Traking enabled. **LINK SCRIPT** Use script tag to add our script on your website. If you have a dedicated console, substitute [API_URL] with the url of your api. For example *https://api.4dem.it/c4rt/js/ect.min.js* [API_URL]/c4rt/js/ect.min.js **COOKIE NAME** ectDem **COOKIE PARAMETERS** { "id": 23, "mode": "campaign" }

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated subscribers.

You can filter for a specific subscriber by `id`, `email_address`, `mobile`, `bounce_type` and `subscription_status` properties.

For `subscription_status` property you should pass `Subscribed` or `Unsubscribed` or `Opt-In Pending` or `Opt-Out Pending`.
For `bounce_type` property you should pass `Hard` or `Soft` or `Not Bounced`.

Examples:
1. filter subscribers for `id` equal to `123`: set `search` equal to `id:123`,
2. filter subscribers for `email_address` equal to `email@example.it`: set `search` equal to `email_address:email@example.it`,
3. filter subscribers for `mobile` equal to `+3933312345645`: set `search` equal to `mobile:+3933312345645`,
4. filter subscribers for `mobile` equal to `+3933312345645` or `email_address` equal to `email@example.it`: set `search` equal to `mobile:+3933312345645;email_address:email@example.it`,
5. filter subscribers for `mobile` equal to `+3933312345645` and `email_address` equal to `email@example.it`: set `search` equal to `mobile:+3933312345645;email_address:email@example.it` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
6. filter subscribers for `bounce_type` equal to `Hard`: set `search` equal to `bounce_type:Hard`,
7. filter subscribers for `bounce_type` equal to `Hard` or `Soft`: set `search` equal to `bounce_type:Hard,Soft`,
8. filter subscribers for `subscription_status` equal to `Unsubscribed`: set `search` equal to `subscription_status:Unsubscribed`,
9. filter subscribers for `subscription_status` equal to `Unsubscribed` or `Subscribed`: set `search` equal to `subscription_status:Unsubscribed,Subscribed`,
10. filter subscribers for `subscription_status` equal to `Unsubscribed` and `bounce_type` equal to `Hard`: set `search` equal to `subscription_status:Unsubscribed;bounce_type:Hard`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

Use this endpoint to get paginated custom fields.

You can filter for a specific custom field by `required`, `unique`, `name` and `type` properties.

For `required` and `unique` properties you should pass `Yes` or `No` as values instead of `true` or `false`.

Examples:

1. filter custom fields for `type` equal to `Single Line`: set `search` equal to `type:Single Line`,
2. filter custom fields for `type` equal to `Single Line` or `Checkboxes`: set `search` equal to `type:Single Line,Checkboxes` and set `searchFields` equal to `type:in`
3. filter custom fields for `type` equal to `Single Line` or `Checkboxes` and `required` field: set `search` equal to `type:Single Line,Checkboxes;required:Yes` and set `searchFields` equal to `type:in;required:=` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`

parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated imports.

You can filter for a specific import by `entity_id`, `entity_type` and `status` properties.

For `entity_type` property you should pass `suppression` or `recipient` as value.
For `status` property you should pass one of these values `draft`, `ready`, `processing`, `done`, `failed`.
For `entity_id` you should pass the recipient_id or 0 for suppression.

Examples:

1. filter imports for `entity_type` equal to `suppression`: set `search` equal to `entity_type:suppression`,
2. filter imports for `entity_type` equal to `recipient`: set `search` equal to `entity_type:recipient`,
3. filter imports for `status` equal to `done`: set `search` equal to `status:done`,
4. filter imports for `status` equal to `done` or `failed`: set `search` equal to `status:done,failed` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in`
5. filter imports for `status` equal to `done` or `failed` and `entity_type` equal to `suppression` field: set `search` equal to `status:done,failed;entity_type:suppression` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in;entity_type:=` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a new import in draft. 
        The focus of the import is defined by the source. 
        Through the `import_strategy` property you can define from which type of source you want to import data. You can import data from `file`, `url` or `db` (mysql). 
        Based on the type of `import_strategy` it is necessary to define the related `import_strategy_settings`.

***From `url`*** 

You can import data from a JSON Api. 

Below is an example of `import_strategy_settings` with `import_strategy` from url.
        
    {
        "url": "http://your-source-data-url.com",
        "is_paginated": true,
        "timeout": 10000,
        "path": "data",
        "page_value": 1,
        "page_key": "page",
        "limit_value": 15,
        "limit_key": "limit",
        "response_page_key": "meta.current_page",
        "responst_total_page_key": "meta.total_pages",
        "responst_total_page_value": 1,
    }
        

***From `db`*** 

You can import data from a mysql database.

Below is an example of `import_strategy_settings` with `import_strategy` from db.
        
    {
        "host": "83.45.49.15 or http://my-db-data.com",
        "port": 3306,
        "database_name": "my_db",
        "username": "my_username",
        "password": "my_password",
        "table_name": "my_table_users",
        "condition": "",
        "columns": ["*"],
        "limit": 100,
    }
        
Note: username and password will be encrypted using a 256-bit security system. We will never save your credentials in clear text on our database. For this reason we cannot show you or retrieve your credentials after they have been saved.

***From `file`*** 

You can import data from a text file or csv. 

In order for you to create an import from file, you need to upload the file to our servers calling this endpoint with content-type multipart/form-data. 

Below is an example of `import_strategy_settings` with `import_strategy` from file.

    {
        "file": "resource-file | mimes:csv,txt | mimeTypes:text/plain,text/csv | max 30 MB",
        "quote": "oneOf:empty,double,single",
        "delimiter": "oneOf:comma,semicolon,tab,auto",
        "skip_first_row": "true|false",
    }

put.description: Use this endpoint to update an import in draft. You can only update `settings`, `mapped_fields` and `callback_url` properties. If you need to change `import_strategy` or `import_strategy_settings` you need to create another import.

delete.description: Use this endpoint to delete permanently an import. Please note that you can't stop a processing import by deleting it.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get information about contacts activities.

You can filter activities by `last_timestamp` using greater than (>) or less than (<) conditions.

Examples:

1. Filter activities where the last activity timestamp is greater than `1696404432`: set `search` equal to `last_timestamp:1696404432` and `searchFields` equal to `last_timestamp:>`,
2. Filter activities where the last activity timestamp is less than `1696404432`: set `search` equal to `last_timestamp:1696404432` and `searchFields` equal to `last_timestamp:<`,

These filters use the `search` parameter to specify the timestamp and `searchFields` to define the condition.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Set limit of the result to return

parameters.name: searchFields
parameters.description: Defines the condition for timestamp comparison (either 'last_timestamp:>' or 'last_timestamp:<')

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated email senders.

You can filter for a specific sender by `email`, `name`, `status` and `dkim.enabled` properties.

For `status` property you should pass `Enabled` or `Disabled`.
For `dkim.enabled` property you should pass `1` or `0` as values instead `true` or `false`.

Examples:

1. filter senders for `status` equal to `Enabled`: set `search` equal to `status:Enabled`,
2. filter senders for `email` like to `my-email`: set `search` equal to `email:my-email`
3. filter senders for `name` like to `my-name`: set `search` equal to `email:my-name`
4. filter senders for `email` like to `my-email` and for `name` like to `my-name`: set `search` equal to `email:my-email;name:my-name` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
5. filter senders who have dkim enabled: set `search` equal to `dkim.enabled:1`,
6. filter senders who not have dkim enabled: set `search` equal to `dkim.enabled:0`,
8. get senders with dkim data: set `include` equal to `dkim`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Set 'dkim' to include dkim information on sender. If dkim is not present, the dkim property is missing on sender data.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create an email sender. 
You will need to confirm the email address used during creation.

Once you receive the confirmation code on email address of sender, use it to call the POST endpoint `senders/email/:senderId/confirm` to confirm the email address. Once you have confirmed the email address you can use it to send email marketing communications.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get sender email by id

put.description:
Use this endpoint to update an email sender information. 
If you change the sender's email address, the system will set it to a disabled state as you will have to reconfirm the new email address (you will have to use the endpoints to send the confirmation email and to confirm the address by passing the code received).

Furthermore, if there is an enabled dkim and the domain of the new email address is different from the old one, the system will check the new domain. If the dkim check fails, the system will deactivate the dkim.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

delete.description: Use this endpoint to delete permanently an email sender.

post.description: Use this endpoint to confirm sender email address.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

post.description: Use this endpoint to verify Dkim on sender email address

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

post.description: Use this endpoint to activate Dkim on sender email address. If domain of email address is valid, the system create and enabled a new dkim on email sender. If dkim is already present, the system enable dkim.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

post.description: Use this endpoint to verify Spf on sender email address.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated sms senders.

You can filter for a specific sender by `status`, `alias`, `confirmed` properties.

For `status` property you should pass `Enabled`, `Disabled` or `Approval` as values.
For `confirmed` property you should pass `1` or `0` as values.

Examples:

1. filter senders for `status` equal to `Enabled`: set `search` equal to `status:Enabled`,
2. filter senders for `alias` like to `my-alias`: set `search` equal to `alias:my-alias`
3. filter senders for `alias` like to `my-alias` and for `status` equal to `Enabled`: set `search` equal to `alias:my-alias;status:Enabled` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
4. filter senders for `confirmed` equal to `1`: set `search` equal to `confirmed:1`,
5. filter senders for `confirmed` equal to `0`: set `search` equal to `confirmed:0`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a sms sender. 
Once the SMS sender has been created, our systems will evaluate whether the sender complies with AGCOM requirements and codes of conduct.
This operation can last from 20 minutes up to 24 hours.

In addition to verifying the requirements, you will need to confirm the mobile number used during creation.
This process involves an API call to the POST endpoint `senders/sms/:senderId/send-activation-code` in order to send the confirmation code to the mobile number defined in the `phone_number` property. Make sure you have at least 1 SMS credit.

Once you receive the confirmation code, use it to call the POST endpoint `senders/sms/:senderId/confirm` to confirm the phone number. Once you have confirmed the telephone number and received approval from AGCOM you can use it to send SMS campaigns or single SMS.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

get.description: Use this endpoint to get sender by id

delete.description: Use this endpoint to delete permanently an sms sender.

post.description: Use this endpoint to confirm a sms sender.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to send a sms message with auth code confirmation. 
You will receive an SMS at the phone number specified when creating the SMS sender.
The message will contain a confirmation code that you will need to use to confirm the sender.

500.description: Internal server error. Please report it.

get.description:
Return records from the suppression list.

Query could be done using those parameters:

    search : define the key to search

    searchFields:operator : define where to search the key (email_address, source, recipient_id) and the operator (like or = )

    eg: get all spam complaints:

    search=Spam%20Complaints&SearchFields=source

    multiple operand could be used through semicolon (;)

**Conducting research in the repository**:

    suppressions?search=email@domain.it
    or
    suppressions?search=domain.it&searchFields=email_address:like
    or
    suppressions?search=email@domain.it&searchFields=email_address:=
    or
    suppressions?search=recipient_id:124;email_address:email@domain.it
    or
    suppressions?search=mobile:+390112234;email_address:email@domain.it&searchFields=mobile:like;email_address:=

**Sorting the results**

    suppressions?orderBy=id&sortedBy=desc

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: type here name of the field

get.summary: Get suppression list grouped per source
get.description:
Return total of suppressions with relative source.

**Sorting the results**

    suppressions/sources?sortedBy=desc

parameters.name: sortedBy
parameters.description: Set ascending or descending order per total

get.summary: Get suppression list grouped per domain
get.description:
Return total of suppressions with relative domain.

**Sorting the results**

    suppressions/domains?orderBy=domain&sortedBy=desc
    suppressions/domains?orderBy=total&sortedBy=desc

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: set order by total (default) or domain

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated wa templates.

You can filter for a specific template by `name` and `status` properties.

For `name` property you should pass a string to search in template name proprerty.
For `status` property you should pass one of these values `pending`, `rejected`, `approved`.

Examples:

1. filter wa templates for `name` like to `customerSupport`: set `search` equal to `name:customerSupport`,
3. filter imports for `status` equal to `pending`: set `search` equal to `status:pending`,
4. filter imports for `status` equal to `approved` or `rejected`: set `search` equal to `status:approved,rejected` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in`
5. filter imports for `status` equal to `approved` or `rejected` and `name` equal to `customerSupport` field: set `search` equal to `status:approved,rejected;name:customerSupport` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in;name:=` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`

parameters.name: searchJoin
parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a new template.

Once the template has been created, it is necessary to wait for approval from Meta. Our systems will wait for approval and change the status of the template based on the response received from Meta. 

However, after approval of the template, it is necessary to wait approximately 20 minutes before using the template for the new status to be propagated on the Meta system.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to get paginated workflows.

You can filter for a specific workflow by `name`, `status` properties.
You can retrieve message data information passing `include` property with `messages`, `recipient` value.

For `status` property you should pass one of these values `draft`, `ready`, `paused`.

Examples:

1. filter workflows for `name` like to `MyWorkflow`: set `search` equal to `name:MyWorkflow`,
3. filter workflows for `status` equal to `ready`: set `search` equal to `status:ready`,
4. filter workflows for `status` equal to `draft` or `ready`: set `search` equal to `status:draft,ready` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in`,
5. filter workflows for `status` equal to `ready` or `draft` and `name` equal to `MyWorkflow` field: set `search` equal to `status:ready,draft;name:MyWorkflow` and set `searchFields` equal to `status:in;name:like` and set `searchJoin` equal to `and`,
     *

parameters.description: Set to 'and' for searching with and logic

parameters.description: Return messages or recipient message data

WorkflowSettings.description:
variable based on trigger_name:

* updateSubscriber
```json
settings: {
    custom_field_ids: [],
    condition: 'almost_one' // 'almost_one', 'all'
}
```

* flowEnd
```json
settings: {
    workflow_id: 123
}
```

* campaignActivity
```json
settings: {
    activity_type: 'click', // 'open', 'click'
    campaign_id: 123,
    value: 'url'
}
```

* onDate
```json
settings: {
    date_source: 'subscriptionDate', // 'subscriptionDate', 'customField'
    repeat_annually: false,
    custom_field_id: 123
}
```

* webSite
```json
settings: {
    type: 'pageview', // 'event', 'pageview'
    value: ''
}
```

* ecommerceAbbandonedCart
```json
settings: {
    value: 3 // min 3 max 24
}
```

* ecommerceProductPurchased
```json
settings: {
    value: ''
}
```

* ecommerceCategoryPurchased
```json
settings: {
    category_type: 'category', // 'category_upper', 'category_med', 'category'
    value: ''
}
```

* ecommerceBrandPurchased
```json
settings: {
    value: ''
}
```

* ecommerceOrderCompleted
```json
settings: {
    operator: 'greater', // 'greater', 'smaller'
    value: 1
}
```

* ecommerceActivityHistory
```json
settings: {
    type: 'amountSpentOnPeriod', // 'amountSpentOnPeriod', 'numberOfProductPurchasedOnPeriod', 'numberOfOrderCompletedOnPeriod'
    operator: 'greater', // 'greater', 'smaller'
    period: 'last_week', // 'last_week', 'last_month', 'last_three_month', 'last_six_month', 'last_year', 'ever'
    value: 1
}
```

 *

priority.description: used to order not 'onDate' workflow messages and is autocalculated

from.description: not used in 'onDate' workflows and depends on before message based on priority

allSegmentsOccurrences.description: if true all segments must be true

bearerAuth.description: Bearer auth token provided by apiKey authentication
